results.title: A Share in their tomorrow
results.brief_description: How a group of British companies are assisting, by aerial surveys, the Sudan Government to open up and develop unproductive, and previously inaccessible, terrain in the Sudan. The valuable contribution of the helicopter for surveying purposes is illustrated.

results.title: A Sense of the other (TEFC)
results.creator_credit: Dana H. Hodgdon, Frank Galati, Bernice Gregorio and Northwestern University
results.brief_description: Shows how old English literature, such as Shakespeare, can be studied through the medium of interpretation. This is understanding the text through performing and acting out the parts, by students. Shows performances, and also discusses with students and lecturers, the value of this method of study.

results.title: A Secret place
results.creator_credit: Kim Cardow, Susan Jones, Nicholas Oughton and College of Art
results.brief_description: Shows the outdoor play environment at the Lady Gowrie Child Centre in Brisbane where children can take advantage of conditions not always available. Tree-houses, streams, mud pools and a variety of animals combine to make this natural environment. Shows how children can interact with even the simplest part of this environment.

results.title: A Rock climb
results.creator_credit: Boy Scouts of America. Visual Education Service
results.brief_description: The film describes the activities of a rock-climbing Explorer Unit from Phoenix, Arizona - the Kachinas. First their equipment and techniques are demonstrated and then the camera records the actual climb of a sheer peak in the Arizona Desert, a peak never before climbed by man.

results.title: A Question of Balance
results.brief_description: This program looks at the issue of woodchipping and forestry management in Australia, with special emphasis on the South East Forest region of Victoria and NSW, and asks the question…What’s it worth to disrupt the balance? With the advent of the Greenhouse Effect it is now imperative that we look carefully and critically at how we manage our environment.

results.title: A Pride of islands
results.brief_description: Visiting the island of the Hebrides showing how they fish, farm and weave to make a living. The only airport in the world to the covered by water twice a day. The old battlements, castles and monuments 3000 years old. Safe haven from the sea for sailors of all nations.

results.title: A Practical guide to worm farming
results.brief_description: This video contains practical information on setting up a commercial worm farm, including: Pros and cons of different bed construction, worm biology/why worms, pests - including flatworms, common problems and how to overcome them, bedding, feeds, covers and harvesting.

results.title: A Positive approach to the psychiatric patient
results.brief_description: Shows the treatment in Veterans Administration hospital for psychiatric patients who have emerged from acute episodes of mental illness but who are not yet well enough to leave the hospital. Uses a hospital ward unit as the focal setting, and stresses the roles of the nurse aide and physician.

results.title: A Portrait of Katherine Mansfield
results.creator_credit: Julienne Stretton, Sue Kedgely and Marigold Productions
results.brief_description: This is the story of the short and extraordinary life of New Zealand writer Katherine Mansfield. Katherine Mansfield was one of the great short stories writers of the English language. Her work is still studied in over twenty languages.
